channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Australian designers giving up on getting Shein copies removed
item.description: Small Australian fashion designers say it is too time-consuming to continue to ask Shein to take down copies of their designs.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Things You Really Like designer Klaudia Burzynska found her design being sold on Shein.

item.title: Live: Rubio says US will continue to target drones and boats that pose a risk
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has defended itself against more strikes, as the US tries to clear safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Iran says it is preventing unauthorised vessels from transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Phone books are still being delivered but does anyone use them?
item.description: Once a must-have household item, are phone books destined to go the way of the answering machine and rotary dial phone?
item.dc:creator: Coco Veldkamp and Jeremy Lee

media:group.media:description: The first Yellow Pages directory in Australia was published in the early 1970s.

item.title: Three men walked into a whisky bar. Eight years on, a court case has ended
item.description: The Salamanca Whisky Bar was accused of selling fake versions of the award-wining Sullivans Cove whisky. But eight years after the saga began, a court in Hobart has dismissed the charges.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Sullivans Cove Distillery became the first distillery outside of Scotland or Japan to win the world's best single malt at the World Whiskies Awards.

item.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia's 'utter cynicism' as strikes kill 22 before ceasefire
item.description: The Ukrainian president called one attack in Poltava "especially vile", accusing Russia of launching a second missile while emergency rescuers were at the scene.

media:group.media:description: The aftermath of a Russian air strike in Zaporizhzhia.

item.title: The Victorian state budget explained in five key charts
item.description: The latest figures released by the government show where its spending is going, and where the money to pay for it all is coming from.
item.dc:creator: Judd Boaz

media:group.media:description: The latest Victorian budget charts the state's finances until the end of the 2020s.

item.title: The most important number isn't in Victoria's state budget pages
item.description: The most important number in the Victorian budget isn't listed in its pages. It's the 206 days until the polls, with the Labor Government seeking an unprecedented fourth term.

media:group.media:description: Victorian Treasurer Jaclyn Symes addresses the state budget lock-up today.

item.title: Farrer contest could remake the conservative side of politics
item.description: The first electoral test for both new coalition party leaders, Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan, sees the opposition at serious risk of losing Farrer for the first time since the seat was created in 1949.
item.dc:creator: Casey Briggs

media:group.media:description: The first electoral test for Matt Canavan and Angus Taylor sees the opposition at serious risk of losing Farrer for the first time since 1949.

item.title: No easy way out: Businesses need to raise prices or put Australians out of work
item.description: Australia had an inflation problem before the Iran war, but it's turned sinister. We've become hostage to oil prices, and as the governor of the Reserve Bank puts it: "We are all feeling poorer".
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: The RBA has raised the cash rate by 25 basis points for the third time this year.

item.title: 'Straw that broke the camel's back': Financial distress grows as rates rise
item.description: Almost 14,000 Australians rang the National Debt Helpline last month, with most callers saying they are struggling to pay their home loans and are facing mortgage stress, according to new data.
item.dc:creator: Nassim Khadem and Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Since the start of the year, more than 65,000 people have contacted the National Debt Helpline in financial distress.

item.title: More than 8 million Australians now receive income support
item.description: The number of people accessing income support payments has risen by 2 million in the past decade, with more Australians unable to work due to deteriorating mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Bronwyn Herbert

media:group.media:description: Laura Frahamer, 30, was unable to continue full time work due to Long COVID.

item.title: How a flamboyant thief quietly pulled off an extraordinary coin heist
item.description: David Gee pulled off one of the most baroque and ambitious coin heists in Australian history, nicking precious coins from the special collections of the nation's oldest library without anybody noticing.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Crabb and Julia O'Shea

media:group.media:description: State Library of NSW veteran librarian Maggie Patten holds up one of the Australian gold coins forger David Gee was obsessed with.

item.title: Quiz: Fancy yourself a jetsetter? Test your knowledge
item.description: Which country does the alcoholic spirit cachaça hail from? And how much do you know about Australian towns with Big Koala statues?

media:group.media:description: A drink on a bar.

item.title: This comic wowed Edinburgh Fringe. Now she's getting a handle on love
item.description: Last year, Elouise Eftos became the first Australian woman to land a best newcomer nod at Edinburgh Fringe. Now, she's got a new show about never falling in love.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story

media:group.media:description: After seasons in Adelaide, Melbourne and Perth, Elouise Eftos brings her new show Aphrodite to Sydney and Brisbane comedy festivals.

item.title: UK police probe arson at ex-synagogue as PM decries antisemitism 'crisis'
item.description: The blaze is the latest in a spate of incidents that appear to have targeted the UK's Jewish community.

media:group.media:description: CounterterrorISM police are investigating the latest blaze in east London.

item.title: Romania's pro-EU government collapses after no-confidence vote
item.description: The country's currency falls to a record low against the euro as concerns mount about the state of its finances.

media:group.media:description: Ousted Romanian PM Ilie Bolojan is accusing opposition parties of lacking an alternative plan to tackle the country's deficit problems.

item.title: Singapore approves caning for school bullies
item.description: The move is part of Singapore's new anti-bullying measures introduced by the Ministry of Education.

media:group.media:description: New standardised anti-bullying measures have been rolled out across schools in Singapore.

item.title: Labor axes funding for $45b Inland Rail project linking Melbourne to Brisbane
item.description: The federal government has halted plans for the Melbourne to Brisbane Inland Rail, amid concerns the project could cost more than $45 billion to complete.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Jeuniewic

media:group.media:description: The Inland Rail project will no longer be funded to connect Melbourne to Brisbane.

item.title: Human-to-human transmission suspected in deadly hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization says no rats were found on board the cruise vessel where the outbreak has taken hold, with three people having died of confirmed or suspected infections of the rat-borne virus.

media:group.media:description: Two cases of hantavirus have been confirmed from passengers who spent time aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Indigenous commissioner condemns comedian's 'deeply offensive' video
item.description: South Australia's Commissioner for Aboriginal Children and Young People speaks out against a "deeply offensive" social media video posted by comedian Alex "Shooter" Williamson in the wake of the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

media:group.media:description: Dale Agius has condemned the comedian's social media post. File photo.

item.title: Teaching positions, courses to go under TasTAFE cuts
item.description: TAFE teaching jobs and courses will go and consideration given to asset sales under a Tasmanian government plan to find savings.
item.dc:creator: Loretta Lohberger

media:group.media:description: Among the job losses are 19 teaching positions, four casual teaching positions, two permanent prison training officers and a fixed-term prison training officer.

item.title: Inquest into cold case hears man who hid teenager's body also lied to police
item.description: Rebecca Richardson was a beloved teenager who went missing from Gin Gin in 1998. Her body has never been found and her family is desperate for answers.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Hines

media:group.media:description: Rebecca Richardson, 15, was last seen at Gin Gin in November 1998.

item.title: '24/7 wanding searches' of patients in hospital's behavioural assessment unit
item.description: Patients entering Canberra Hospital's Behavioural Assessment Unit will now undergo metal detector screening after a knife incident forced staff into a three-hour lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Lois Maskiell

media:group.media:description: All patients to Canberra Hospital's Behavioural Assessment Unit are now being searched with metal detection wands.

item.title: Childcare worker granted bail over alleged 'brutal assault' of children
item.description: Jayendra Shahi was charged with allegedly assaulting two girls at a childcare centre in Sydney's south-west.
item.dc:creator: Sean Tarek Goodwin

media:group.media:description: Jayendra Shahi was granted bail on Tuesday.

item.title: 'Good luck walking home': Alice Springs murder trial centres on intent
item.description: Jurors in a trial over the alleged murder of an Alice Springs teenager have heard competing arguments about how to interpret videos taken in the lead up to the young man's death.
item.dc:creator: Chris Murrkarany Fitzpatrick

media:group.media:description: Yiel Gatluak, 19, was found dead on a dirt road outside Alice Springs in 2024, with three people now facing trial charged with his murder.
